missions.summary: The first artificial satellite. A 58 cm aluminum sphere with four radio antennas, audible worldwide on shortwave for 22 days.
missions.impact: Started the Space Race and the entire field of practical spaceflight.

missions.summary: Single 108-minute orbit of Earth by Cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin - humanity's first journey beyond the atmosphere.
missions.impact: Demonstrated humans could survive launch, weightlessness and re-entry.

missions.summary: First humans to leave low Earth orbit, the first to see the far side of the Moon, and the first to take the Earthrise photograph.
missions.impact: Proved the trans-lunar trajectory and gave humanity its first 'planet portrait' photo.

missions.summary: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on another world; Michael Collins orbited above.
missions.impact: Fulfilled the 1961 Kennedy goal and showed crewed planetary exploration was possible.

missions.status: Mission ended 2003 - first spacecraft beyond Pluto's orbit
missions.summary: First spacecraft to traverse the asteroid belt and reach Jupiter. Carried the famous Pioneer plaque message to extraterrestrials.
missions.impact: Showed the asteroid belt was not a fatal hazard and opened outer-planet exploration.

missions.summary: Visited Jupiter (1979) and Saturn (1980), then continued outward. Crossed the heliopause in August 2012 - the first human-made object in interstellar space.
missions.impact: Carries the Golden Record - humanity's message to the cosmos.

missions.summary: The only spacecraft ever to fly past Uranus (1986) and Neptune (1989). Discovered 16 new moons and 2 new rings in those flybys.
missions.impact: Our entire close-up knowledge of Uranus and Neptune still comes from this single mission.

missions.summary: The first major optical telescope in space. After 1993's servicing mission corrected its mirror, it transformed cosmology, exoplanet science and public imagination.
missions.impact: 1.5 million observations; supports virtually every active astrophysicist's research.

missions.summary: Modular orbital laboratory. The largest peacetime international engineering project in human history.
missions.impact: Continuous human presence in space for 25+ years; biological & materials research.

missions.status: Completed - Grand Finale dive into Saturn
missions.summary: 13 years orbiting Saturn. Deployed the Huygens probe to Titan - the only landing in the outer solar system.
missions.impact: Discovered active geysers on Enceladus, methane lakes on Titan, and revolutionized understanding of ring dynamics.

missions.summary: Searching for signs of ancient microbial life. Caching samples for return by a future mission. Carries the Ingenuity helicopter - the first powered flight on another planet.
missions.impact: Most advanced astrobiology rover yet; samples will return to Earth in the 2030s.

missions.target: Sun-Earth L2 (1.5 million km from Earth)
missions.summary: 6.5 m segmented infrared telescope, 100× more powerful than Hubble for some observations.
missions.impact: First operational year resolved the deepest infrared image of the universe and observed atmospheres of exoplanets.

missions.summary: Uncrewed test flight of NASA's SLS rocket and Orion crew vehicle. Sent Orion on a 25-day distant retrograde orbit around the Moon - the first step in returning humans to the lunar surface.
missions.impact: Validated the SLS / Orion stack ahead of crewed missions (Artemis II onwards).
missions.target: Distant Retrograde Orbit around the Moon

missions.status: Completed primary; extended cruise to asteroid 1998 KY26 (arrival 2031)
missions.summary: Rendezvoused with the carbonaceous near-Earth asteroid Ryugu in 2018, deployed three rovers and a lander, fired a copper impactor to expose subsurface material, and returned 5.4 g of pristine samples to Earth in December 2020.
missions.impact: First mission to deliver subsurface asteroid material to Earth; samples revealed organic compounds and water-bearing minerals predating the Solar System.

missions.target: Asteroid 101955 Bennu (sample return); now OSIRIS-APEX → Apophis
missions.status: Sample capsule recovered Sept 2023; spacecraft renamed OSIRIS-APEX en route to Apophis (2029 encounter)
missions.summary: First U.S. asteroid sample-return mission. Touched the rubble-pile asteroid Bennu in October 2020 and brought 121.6 g back to the Utah desert in September 2023 - the largest extraterrestrial sample since Apollo.
missions.impact: Revealed Bennu carries water-bearing clays and organic carbon - building blocks of life delivered to early Earth.

missions.status: Active - completed first ever flight through the solar corona (Dec 2024, 6.9 million km perihelion)
missions.summary: First spacecraft designed to fly through the Sun's corona, protected by a 11.4 cm carbon-composite heat shield. Uses Venus gravity assists to spiral inward, reaching speeds above 690,000 km/h - fastest human-made object ever.
missions.impact: Directly sampling the solar wind at its source; resolved the long-standing coronal heating problem and Alfvénic switchbacks.

missions.target: Von Kármán crater, lunar far side
missions.summary: First soft landing on the far side of the Moon, enabled by the Queqiao relay satellite at the Earth–Moon L2 point. Yutu-2 rover continues to traverse a region permanently shielded from Earth-side radio noise.
missions.impact: Opened the lunar far side to surface science - including the first low-frequency radio astronomy from a place where Earth's noise cannot reach.

missions.status: Completed - first successful planetary defense test
missions.summary: Kinetic impactor that struck the 160 m moonlet Dimorphos at 6.1 km/s, deliberately altering its orbital period around Didymos by 33 minutes - far more than the 73-second mission threshold.
missions.impact: Proved humanity can deflect a hazardous asteroid. ESA's Hera mission (launched 2024) will arrive in 2026 to characterize the impact crater in detail.

missions.summary: First dedicated mission to study Jupiter's icy moon Europa. Carries 9 science instruments to characterize its subsurface ocean.
missions.impact: Could give the most definitive evidence yet of a habitable extraterrestrial ocean.
